[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2024-12-09
[TITLE]: A boat goes up in flames in Elliott Bay, one person rescued – KIRO 7 News Seattle
[METADATA/INTRO]: SEATTLE — A thick plume of black smoke was visible for miles after boat fire in Elliott Bay off the shore in West Seattle. The US Coast Guard has confirmed that one person was on board but was not injured....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: SEATTLE — A thick plume of black smoke was visible for miles after boat fire in Elliott Bay off the shore in West Seattle. 
 The US Coast Guard has confirmed that one person was on board but was not injured. 
 According to officials at the scene, a private boat got the person out of the water. 
 The person pulled from the water got a hot shower and declined medical treatment. 
 No one was hurt. 
 “It was black smoke, way up in the air,” said Don Mann, who captured the chaos on camera. 
 He and his wife walk along this shore every single day. They’ve never seen anything like it.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Boat fire near James River Bridge causes multi-agency response | 13newsnow.com
[METADATA/INTRO]: NEWPORT NEWS, Va. — A boat fire near the James River Bridge stopped traffic on the bridge in both directions Sunday morning. Newport News Assistant Fire Chief Dave Lewis confirmed a working boat fire near the bridge around 10 a.m....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Petty Officer Noel said six people were taken off the boat and brought to shore. Three people reportedly suffered burn injuries, although it is not immediately clear how severe they are. 
 Amie Wise, who was sitting in her truck at Huntington Beach Park, noticed a big cloud of black smoke rising from the direction of the bridge. 
 "I mean, it was pretty big. I would say it went a few hundred feet up in the air, and you could see every so often it would kind of get like really, really, really dark black, big clouds of smoke," she said.

[TITLE]: Boat fire on Lake Minnetonka spurs warning from officials | kare11.com
[METADATA/INTRO]: LONG LAKE, Minn. — Three people were forced to jump into Lake Minnetonka after their boat started on fire near Smith's Bay on Sunday evening, according to the Long Lake Fire Department. Fire Chief James Van Eyll says the three...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: LONG LAKE, Minn. — Three people were forced to jump into Lake Minnetonka after their boat started on fire near Smith's Bay on Sunday evening, according to the Long Lake Fire Department. 
 Fire Chief James Van Eyll says the three had been on the lake most of the day, and when they tried to start the boat to go back in to shore they heard a pop, and then saw smoke and flames. 
 The boaters jumped from the burning vessel into the lake and then swam to shore and called 911. 
 Long Lake firefighters worked to extinguish the boat on the water, but also laid water lines on nearby docks in case the burning boat drifted them. After the fire had been put down the boat was towed to a local marina so insurance investigators can go through it.
